raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 5,
    "R1.7": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The agent maps against the nearest 3-5 works (CAMEL, Generative Agents, ReAct, HuggingGPT) and identifies both sharp and rhetorical deltas, such as the distinction between role-playing societies, behavior-grounded simulations, and tool-augmented controllers.",
    "R1.7": "The agent audits every load-bearing citation, flags missing key works (MetaGPT, ChatDev), and provides bibliographic precision for each cited work, including year and authorship.",
    "RX.1": "For each recommendation, the agent marks confidence levels and identifies missing evidence or areas that need further substantiation, such as the central limitation claim and the novelty of typed inter-agent communication.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) dimensions without any drift, addressing all requested improvements within the scope of the draft.",
    "RX.4": "The agent avoids introducing new errors or harmful content and identifies potential framing risks that could be problematic in the original text.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, providing clear traceability for all revisions made to the draft."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.9,
    "R1.7": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.95,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The agent maps against the nearest 3-5 works (CAMEL, Generative Agents, ReAct, HuggingGPT) and identifies both sharp and rhetorical deltas, such as the distinction between role-playing societies, behavior-grounded simulations, and tool-augmented controllers.
rationale.R1.7: The agent audits every load-bearing citation, flags missing key works (MetaGPT, ChatDev), and provides bibliographic precision for each cited work, including year and authorship.
rationale.RX.1: For each recommendation, the agent marks confidence levels and identifies missing evidence or areas that need further substantiation, such as the central limitation claim and the novelty of typed inter-agent communication.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) dimensions without any drift, addressing all requested improvements within the scope of the draft.
rationale.RX.4: The agent avoids introducing new errors or harmful content and identifies potential framing risks that could be problematic in the original text.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, providing clear traceability for all revisions made to the draft.
